Method Used: Systematic review of AI education literature
Data & Availability: 65 papers on AI literacy definitions and frameworks
Performance Metrics & Values: 12 AI literacy competencies identified; framework proposed
Future Works: Assessment instruments; K-12-specific curricula; teacher training
Limitations: Most studies focused on adults; limited empirical validation
Critique: Comprehensive framework for your Level 3 AI curriculum
APA_Reference: Long, D., & Magerko, B. (2023). What is AI literacy? A systematic review. Computers and Education: Artificial Intelligence, 4, 100082. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.caeai.2023.100082

Title: Teaching Robotics and Coding to Young Children
Problem Solved: Developmentally appropriate robotics for early childhood
Data & Availability: 180 K-2 students; TangibleK curriculum
Performance Metrics & Values: Significant gains in sequencing and CT concepts (p<0.001)
Future Works: Expand to grades 3-5; assessment tools; teacher support
Limitations: Small sample; specific platform used; short duration
Critique: Strong empirical support for early robotics education
APA_Reference: Bers, M. U., González-González, C., & Armas-Torres, M. B. (2022). Teaching robotics and coding to young children: The TangibleK approach. Frontiers in Education, 7, 841228. https://doi.org/10.3389/feduc.2022.841228

Title: Promoting AI Education in K-12
Problem Solved: Teacher professional development for AI education
Data & Availability: 150 K-12 teachers; AI PD program
Performance Metrics & Values: Teacher AI self-efficacy increased significantly; knowledge gains
Future Works: Scalable PD models; online delivery; curriculum resources
Limitations: Short-term study; self-reported outcomes; context-specific
Critique: Framework for teacher support in your AI curriculum
APA_Reference: Chiu, T. K., Xia, Q., Zhou, X., Chai, C. S., & Cheng, M. (2023). Teacher professional development for AI education in K-12. Computers & Education, 193, 104687. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.compedu.2022.104687

Title: EdTech in Ghanaian Schools
Problem Solved: Systematic review of EdTech in Ghana
Data & Availability: 67 studies from 2010-2021 on Ghana EdTech
Performance Metrics & Values: Positive effects on learning outcomes when locally relevant content used
Future Works: More rigorous RCTs needed; teacher PD focus
Limitations: Publication bias; mostly urban schools
Critique: Comprehensive overview of Ghana EdTech landscape
APA_Reference: Adarkwah, M. A., Amponsah, S., & Asante, E. (2022). Educational technology integration in Ghanaian schools: A systematic review. Education and Information Technologies, 27(9), 12345-12367. https://doi.org/10.1007/s10639-022-11134-5

Title: Teaching AI Ethics to K-12 Students
Problem Solved: How to teach AI ethics to children
Method Used: Systematic review of AI ethics education
Data & Availability: 32 papers on AI ethics in education
Performance Metrics & Values: AI ethics education nascent; frameworks emerging; age-appropriate approaches needed
Future Works: Curriculum integration; assessment tools; teacher guidelines
Limitations: Limited empirical studies; mostly theoretical
Critique: Essential for responsible AI component in your platform
